parents.mother_research_notes: Elizabeth Lawrence (no I-prefix entry — not built as no Hooker connection).

narrative_blocks.header: Bones bleached on the Wabash, November 1791
narrative_blocks.body: Ordered to charge the enemy with bayonet to cover the retreating army at St Clair's Defeat, Hart and almost every man of his party were killed. The 1819 Connecticut Gazette obituary mourned that 'their bones were left to bleach upon the borders of the waters of the Wabash, the dreary abode of wild beasts.'

narrative_blocks.header: Trade quarrel jailed him until Lexington Alarm
narrative_blocks.body: Before the war he entered into trade with the Kensington minister, fell into a serious quarrel, and was thrown into jail. He remained imprisoned until the Lexington Alarm of 1775 — when the war that would carry him from Yorktown to St Clair's Defeat finally began.

narrative_blocks.header: Thirty Ohio letters guided Connecticut Reserve buyers
narrative_blocks.body: While garrisoned at Fort Washington Ohio in 1791, he wrote some thirty letters to his Farmington friend Maj William Judd about soil, climate, and the value of Ohio lands. The Connecticut Reserve commissioners 'wisely availed themselves of his knowledge' — the Judd descendants preserved the letters in New Britain through 1875.

education.notes: — Yale 1768, with honors. Brigadier General. Killed at St. Clair's Defeat 1791. Cousin of Gen. Selah Hart (H00273, Hooker descendant). Not a direct Hooker descendant — included for community context.

children_noted: I00041 Mercy/Mary Hart — married John Hooker Jr. (H00098) July 4, 1728

sources.notes: Barbour Collection: "Mary, d. Dea. Ebenezer, m. John HOOKER, Jr., July 4, 1728"

INST022.primary_name: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions
INST022.name: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions
INST022.notes: First American foreign-mission organization. Tree connections: Rev. Elias Cornelius (agent 1816, secretary 1831-32); Ainsworth and Harriet Ellsworth Blunt, missionaries to the Cherokee at Brainerd, Tennessee; Eliza Talcott, missionary to Japan; Rev. William Banfield Capron, Madura mission; Judge John Hooker, member.

INST035.name: United States Military Academy (West Point)
INST035.notes: Founded 1802 at West Point NY. HOOKER TREE CONNECTION: Rev. George Washington Woodbridge (HD2163), son of General Jonathan Woodbridge (H00928), graduated West Point; served as 2nd Lieutenant US Army 1826-1829 before entering the Episcopal ministry. He became rector of Monumental Church Richmond VA 1843-1878 and escorted Flora Cooke Stuart to her dying husband Jeb Stuart in May 1864.
